gpt55_review_summary: Franklin the 3rd found that the first screen could imply separate adapter packages, that the new front door still dropped into migration-era restaurant wording, and that internal README shortcut anchors were not fully guarded. The README now says vLLM/SGLang adapter modules ship with cachet-kv, the migration-era opening was replaced with present-tense Cachet product language, and governance tests assert the target headings exist.
verification: local README markdown link check -> all local links resolve
changed-file secret scan for Databricks, GitHub, OpenAI, and private-key patterns -> no matches
Franklin the 3rd review found adapter-package wording, migration-era opening, and anchor-target guard notes; all three were fixed
what_changed: Added a product-first Start Here section to the root README with install/import identity, target hardware, current benchmark status, and human entry points.
Rewrote the immediate root README transition into present-tense Cachet product language instead of migration-era restaurant wording.
Added a .github/README.md pointer back to the root README so GitHub folder readers do not mistake metadata docs for the project front page.
Added governance assertions that pin the root README front-door content, internal anchor targets, and .github pointer.
why: Make the repository root README meaningful for newcomers before they reach release evidence, implementation history, or GitHub metadata folders.
